data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't see it.

The fix: add the generated file to `<Compile>` within the target.
data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The MSBuild `Compile` items are already resolved by the time `CoreCompile` runs, so the generated file isn't included in compilation.

The fix is to add a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` item within the target, after generating the file. This way the generated `.cs` file gets added to the compilation.

      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />"}

data.content:
**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `<Compile>` item group. MSBuild resolves compile items before `CoreCompile` runs, so the generated file was invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
